content:
What could go wrong when governments worldwide are racing to deploy AI in public services?

8 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image source: https://stock.adobe.com/ (Licensed)Not a Medium member? You can read the full article for free at this link: https://medium.com/@umairali.khan/the-risk-of-putting-ai-adoption-before-public-value-1ee8f67c207f?sk=a590f30e482fb9aa264ed554913e6bc2
TL;DR
Governments are moving quickly to automate public services with AI, but faster processes and lower staffing costs do not automatically create better outcomes. The real challenge is deciding which tasks AI can handle reliably, where human judgment must remain, and how public value should be measured before automation is scaled.
A senior Finnish official recently revealed the government’s plan to transform the country’s entire public sector into an AI-based system by 2031, putting it this way:
“AI will essentially replace humans.”
Finland is not the only country embarking on this journey.
The UK has laid out a broad plan for modern digital government and is testing AI across healthcare, education, planning, taxation, and justice. Canada has launched its first AI strategy for the federal public service. Singapore is promoting AI adoption across…
